© by he ational eographic ociety, pril 
he ospel of udas
optic tet established by
odolphe asser and regor urst
he optic transcription made available in this online edition is a preliminary edition of
the optic tet of the ospel of udas from ode chacos. he transcription is dated
to ebruary , and it has served as the basis for the nglish translation published in
the book
he ospel of udas.   dited by odolphe asser, arvin eyer, and regor
urst   with   dditional   ommentary   by   art   .   hrman.   ashington,   ..
ational eographic ociety, .
he on-going work of reconstruction and the emergence of new fragments of ode
chacos, with readings previously available to the editors only in photographs, will lead
to minor tetual changes in the critical edition of ode chacos, which is scheduled to
be published by the ational eographic ociety in the fall of .
etual signs
 a    dot placed beneath a letter indicates that the reading of the letter is very
uncertain.
 …    dot on the line indicates illegible traces of a letter.
 {   }   quare brackets indicate a lacuna in the manuscript. uch a lacuna may be
restored with optic letters within the square brackets, if a restoration of a
lacuna is judged by the editors to be plausible. therwise, dots are placed
within the square   brackets   to   indicate the   approimate   number   of   letters
missing. n the case of larger lacunae, the space between the brackets is left
blank.
 %   &   ointed brackets indicate a correction suggested by the editors of a scribal
omission or error. hile scribal omissions are corrected in the optic tet, a
scribal error will be corrected in a footnote.
 {   }   races indicate letters or words erroneously added by the scribe of the code.
 vac(at)   he atin epression  vacat   “it is void” indicates space left blank by the scribe
of the code.
etters inserted above the manuscript line by the scribe or an ancient corrector are not
presented differently in this preliminary edition. upralinear strokes are reproduced
when attested or visible in the manuscript, and they have been standardized to a
degree with regard to their position. n the case of restored tet between square brackets,
the editors have adopted the system of supralineation used in classic ahidic manuscripts.
